overview.description: The Nageshvara-Chennakeshava temple complex, sometimes referred to as the Nagesvara and Chennakesava temples of Mosale, is a pair of nearly identical Hindu temples in the village of Mosale near Hassan city, Karnataka, India. One for Shiva, other for Vishnu, this pair is a set of highly ornamented stone temples, illustrating the Hoysala architecture. These temples also include panels of artwork related to the goddess tradition of Hinduism (Shaktism) and Vedic deities. faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nageshvara-Chennakeshava Temple complex, Mosale?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nageshvara-Chennakeshava Temple complex, Mosale is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nageshvara-Chennakeshava Temple complex, Mosale?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (93 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (85 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nageshvara-Chennakeshava Temple complex, Mosale at a relaxed pace.
